speaker: FABIAN
text: Now, as thou lov’st me, let me see his letter.

speaker: FOOL
text: Good Master Fabian, grant me another request.

speaker: FABIAN
text: Anything.

speaker: FOOL
text: Do not desire to see this letter.

speaker: FABIAN
text: This is to give a dog and in recompense desire

speaker: FABIAN
text: my dog again.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Belong you to the Lady Olivia, friends?

speaker: FOOL
text: Ay, sir, we are some of her trappings.

speaker: ORSINO
text: I know thee well. How dost thou, my good fellow?

speaker: FOOL
text: Truly, sir, the better for my foes and the worse

speaker: FOOL
text: for my friends.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Just the contrary: the better for thy friends.

speaker: FOOL
text: No, sir, the worse.

speaker: ORSINO
text: How can that be?

speaker: FOOL
text: Marry, sir, they praise me and make an ass of me.

speaker: FOOL
text: Now my foes tell me plainly I am an ass; so that by

speaker: FOOL
text: my foes, sir, I profit in the knowledge of myself, and

speaker: FOOL
text: by my friends I am abused. So that, conclusions to

speaker: FOOL
text: be as kisses, if your four negatives make your two

speaker: FOOL
text: affirmatives, why then the worse for my friends and

speaker: FOOL
text: the better for my foes.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Why, this is excellent.

speaker: FOOL
text: By my troth, sir, no—though it please you to be

speaker: FOOL
text: one of my friends.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Thou shalt not be the worse for me; there’s gold.

speaker: FOOL
text: But that it would be double-dealing, sir, I would

speaker: FOOL
text: you could make it another.

speaker: ORSINO
text: O, you give me ill counsel.

speaker: FOOL
text: Put your grace in your pocket, sir, for this once,

speaker: FOOL
text: and let your flesh and blood obey it.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Well, I will be so much a sinner to be a

speaker: ORSINO
text: double-dealer: there’s another.

speaker: FOOL
text: Primo, secundo, tertio is a good play, and the old

speaker: FOOL
text: saying is, the third pays for all. The triplex, sir, is a

speaker: FOOL
text: good tripping measure, or the bells of Saint Bennet,

speaker: FOOL
text: sir, may put you in mind—one, two, three.

speaker: ORSINO
text: You can fool no more money out of me at this

speaker: ORSINO
text: throw. If you will let your lady know I am here to

speaker: ORSINO
text: speak with her, and bring her along with you, it

speaker: ORSINO
text: may awake my bounty further.

speaker: FOOL
text: Marry, sir, lullaby to your bounty till I come

speaker: FOOL
text: again. I go, sir, but I would not have you to think

speaker: FOOL
text: that my desire of having is the sin of covetousness.

speaker: FOOL
text: But, as you say, sir, let your bounty take a nap. I

speaker: FOOL
text: will awake it anon.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Here comes the man, sir, that did rescue me.

speaker: ORSINO
text: That face of his I do remember well.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Yet when I saw it last, it was besmeared

speaker: ORSINO
text: As black as Vulcan in the smoke of war.

speaker: ORSINO
text: A baubling vessel was he captain of,

speaker: ORSINO
text: For shallow draught and bulk unprizable,

speaker: ORSINO
text: With which such scatheful grapple did he make

speaker: ORSINO
text: With the most noble bottom of our fleet

speaker: ORSINO
text: That very envy and the tongue of loss

speaker: ORSINO
text: Cried fame and honor on him.—What’s the matter?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: Orsino, this is that Antonio

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: That took the Phoenix and her fraught from Candy,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: And this is he that did the Tiger board

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: When your young nephew Titus lost his leg.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: Here in the streets, desperate of shame and state,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              OFFICER
text: In private brabble did we apprehend him.

speaker: VIOLA
text: He did me kindness, sir, drew on my side,

speaker: VIOLA
text: But in conclusion put strange speech upon me.

speaker: VIOLA
text: I know not what ’twas but distraction.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Notable pirate, thou saltwater thief,

speaker: ORSINO
text: What foolish boldness brought thee to their mercies

speaker: ORSINO
text: Whom thou, in terms so bloody and so dear,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Hast made thine enemies?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Orsino, noble sir,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Be pleased that I shake off these names you give me.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Antonio never yet was thief or pirate,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Though, I confess, on base and ground enough,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Orsino’s enemy. A witchcraft drew me hither.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: That most ingrateful boy there by your side

speaker: ANTONIO
text: From the rude sea’s enraged and foamy mouth

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Did I redeem; a wrack past hope he was.

speaker: ANTONIO
text: His life I gave him and did thereto add

speaker: ANTONIO
text: My love, without retention or restraint,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: All his in dedication. For his sake

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Did I expose myself, pure for his love,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Into the danger of this adverse town;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Drew to defend him when he was beset;

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Where, being apprehended, his false cunning

speaker: ANTONIO
text: (Not meaning to partake with me in danger)

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Taught him to face me out of his acquaintance

speaker: ANTONIO
text: And grew a twenty years’ removèd thing

speaker: ANTONIO
text: While one would wink; denied me mine own purse,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Which I had recommended to his use

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Not half an hour before.

speaker: VIOLA
text: How can this be?

speaker: ORSINO
text: When came he to this town?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Today, my lord; and for three months before,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: No int’rim, not a minute’s vacancy,

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Both day and night did we keep company.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Here comes the Countess. Now heaven walks on Earth!—

speaker: ORSINO
text: But for thee, fellow: fellow, thy words are madness.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Three months this youth hath tended upon me—

speaker: ORSINO
text:
But more of that anon.
                 
                To
                 
                an
                 
                Officer
                .
               Take him aside.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: What would my lord, but that he may not have,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Wherein Olivia may seem serviceable?—

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Cesario, you do not keep promise with me.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Madam?

speaker: ORSINO
text: Gracious Olivia—

speaker: OLIVIA
text: What do you say, Cesario?—Good my lord—

speaker: VIOLA
text: My lord would speak; my duty hushes me.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: If it be aught to the old tune, my lord,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: It is as fat and fulsome to mine ear

speaker: OLIVIA
text: As howling after music.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Still so cruel?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Still so constant, lord.

speaker: ORSINO
text: What, to perverseness? You, uncivil lady,

speaker: ORSINO
text: To whose ingrate and unauspicious altars

speaker: ORSINO
text: My soul the faithful’st off’rings have breathed out

speaker: ORSINO
text: That e’er devotion tendered—what shall I do?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Even what it please my lord that shall become him.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Why should I not, had I the heart to do it,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Like to th’ Egyptian thief at point of death,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Kill what I love?—a savage jealousy

speaker: ORSINO
text: That sometime savors nobly. But hear me this:

speaker: ORSINO
text: Since you to nonregardance cast my faith,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And that I partly know the instrument

speaker: ORSINO
text: That screws me from my true place in your favor,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Live you the marble-breasted tyrant still.

speaker: ORSINO
text: But this your minion, whom I know you love,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And whom, by heaven I swear, I tender dearly,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Him will I tear out of that cruel eye

speaker: ORSINO
text: Where he sits crownèd in his master’s spite.—

speaker: ORSINO
text: Come, boy, with me. My thoughts are ripe in mischief.

speaker: ORSINO
text: I’ll sacrifice the lamb that I do love

speaker: ORSINO
text: To spite a raven’s heart within a dove.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And I, most jocund, apt, and willingly,

speaker: VIOLA
text: To do you rest a thousand deaths would die.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Where goes Cesario?

speaker: VIOLA
text: After him I love

speaker: VIOLA
text: More than I love these eyes, more than my life,

speaker: VIOLA
text: More by all mores than e’er I shall love wife.

speaker: VIOLA
text: If I do feign, you witnesses above,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Punish my life for tainting of my love.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Ay me, detested! How am I beguiled!

speaker: VIOLA
text: Who does beguile you? Who does do you wrong?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Hast thou forgot thyself? Is it so long?—

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Call forth the holy father.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Come, away!

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Whither, my lord?—Cesario, husband, stay.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Husband?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Ay, husband. Can he that deny?

speaker: ORSINO
text: Her husband, sirrah?

speaker: VIOLA
text: No, my lord, not I.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Alas, it is the baseness of thy fear

speaker: OLIVIA
text: That makes thee strangle thy propriety.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Fear not, Cesario. Take thy fortunes up.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Be that thou know’st thou art, and then thou art

speaker: OLIVIA
text: As great as that thou fear’st.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: O, welcome, father.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Father, I charge thee by thy reverence

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Here to unfold (though lately we intended

speaker: OLIVIA
text: To keep in darkness what occasion now

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Reveals before ’tis ripe) what thou dost know

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Hath newly passed between this youth and me.

speaker: PRIEST
text: A contract of eternal bond of love,

speaker: PRIEST
text: Confirmed by mutual joinder of your hands,

speaker: PRIEST
text: Attested by the holy close of lips,

speaker: PRIEST
text: Strengthened by interchangement of your rings,

speaker: PRIEST
text: And all the ceremony of this compact

speaker: PRIEST
text: Sealed in my function, by my testimony;

speaker: PRIEST
text: Since when, my watch hath told me, toward my grave

speaker: PRIEST
text: I have traveled but two hours.

speaker: ORSINO
text: O thou dissembling cub! What wilt thou be

speaker: ORSINO
text: When time hath sowed a grizzle on thy case?

speaker: ORSINO
text: Or will not else thy craft so quickly grow

speaker: ORSINO
text: That thine own trip shall be thine overthrow?

speaker: ORSINO
text: Farewell, and take her, but direct thy feet

speaker: ORSINO
text: Where thou and I henceforth may never meet.

speaker: VIOLA
text: My lord, I do protest—

speaker: OLIVIA
text: O, do not swear.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Hold little faith, though thou hast too much fear.

speaker: ANDREW
text: For the love of God, a surgeon! Send one

speaker: ANDREW
text: presently to Sir Toby.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: What’s the matter?

speaker: ANDREW
text: Has broke my head across, and has given Sir

speaker: ANDREW
text: Toby a bloody coxcomb too. For the love of God,

speaker: ANDREW
text: your help! I had rather than forty pound I were at

speaker: ANDREW
text: home.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Who has done this, Sir Andrew?

speaker: ANDREW
text: The Count’s gentleman, one Cesario. We took

speaker: ANDREW
text: him for a coward, but he’s the very devil

speaker: ANDREW
text: incardinate.

speaker: ORSINO
text: My gentleman Cesario?

speaker: ANDREW
text: ’Od’s lifelings, here he is!—You broke my

speaker: ANDREW
text: head for nothing, and that that I did, I was set on to

speaker: ANDREW
text: do ’t by Sir Toby.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Why do you speak to me? I never hurt you.

speaker: VIOLA
text: You drew your sword upon me without cause,

speaker: VIOLA
text: But I bespake you fair and hurt you not.

speaker: ANDREW
text: If a bloody coxcomb be a hurt, you have hurt

speaker: ANDREW
text: me. I think you set nothing by a bloody coxcomb.

speaker: ANDREW
text: Here comes Sir Toby halting. You shall hear

speaker: ANDREW
text: more. But if he had not been in drink, he would

speaker: ANDREW
text: have tickled you othergates than he did.

speaker: ORSINO
text: How now, gentleman? How is ’t with you?

speaker: TOBY
text: That’s all one. Has hurt me, and there’s th’ end

speaker: TOBY
text:
on ’t.
                 
                To
                 
                Fool
                .
               Sot, didst see Dick Surgeon, sot?

speaker: FOOL
text: O, he’s drunk, Sir Toby, an hour agone; his eyes

speaker: FOOL
text: were set at eight i’ th’ morning.

speaker: TOBY
text: Then he’s a rogue and a passy-measures pavin. I

speaker: TOBY
text: hate a drunken rogue.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Away with him! Who hath made this havoc

speaker: OLIVIA
text: with them?

speaker: ANDREW
text: I’ll help you, Sir Toby, because we’ll be

speaker: ANDREW
text: dressed together.

speaker: TOBY
text: Will you help?—an ass-head, and a coxcomb,

speaker: TOBY
text: and a knave, a thin-faced knave, a gull?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Get him to bed, and let his hurt be looked to.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: I am sorry, madam, I have hurt your kinsman,

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: But, had it been the brother of my blood,

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: I must have done no less with wit and safety.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: You throw a strange regard upon me, and by that

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: I do perceive it hath offended you.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Pardon me, sweet one, even for the vows

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: We made each other but so late ago.

speaker: ORSINO
text: One face, one voice, one habit, and two persons!

speaker: ORSINO
text: A natural perspective, that is and is not!

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Antonio, O, my dear Antonio!

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: How have the hours racked and tortured me

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Since I have lost thee!

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Sebastian are you?

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Fear’st thou that, Antonio?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: How have you made division of yourself?

speaker: ANTONIO
text: An apple cleft in two is not more twin

speaker: ANTONIO
text: Than these two creatures. Which is Sebastian?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Most wonderful!

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Do I stand there? I never had a brother,

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Nor can there be that deity in my nature

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Of here and everywhere. I had a sister

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Whom the blind waves and surges have devoured.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Of charity, what kin are you to me?

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: What countryman? What name? What parentage?

speaker: VIOLA
text: Of Messaline. Sebastian was my father.

speaker: VIOLA
text: Such a Sebastian was my brother too.

speaker: VIOLA
text: So went he suited to his watery tomb.

speaker: VIOLA
text: If spirits can assume both form and suit,

speaker: VIOLA
text: You come to fright us.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: A spirit I am indeed,

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: But am in that dimension grossly clad

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Which from the womb I did participate.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Were you a woman, as the rest goes even,

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: I should my tears let fall upon your cheek

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: And say “Thrice welcome, drownèd Viola.”

speaker: VIOLA
text: My father had a mole upon his brow.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: And so had mine.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And died that day when Viola from her birth

speaker: VIOLA
text: Had numbered thirteen years.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: O, that record is lively in my soul!

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: He finishèd indeed his mortal act

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: That day that made my sister thirteen years.

speaker: VIOLA
text: If nothing lets to make us happy both

speaker: VIOLA
text: But this my masculine usurped attire,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Do not embrace me till each circumstance

speaker: VIOLA
text: Of place, time, fortune, do cohere and jump

speaker: VIOLA
text: That I am Viola; which to confirm,

speaker: VIOLA
text: I’ll bring you to a captain in this town,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Where lie my maiden weeds; by whose gentle help

speaker: VIOLA
text: I was preserved to serve this noble count.

speaker: VIOLA
text: All the occurrence of my fortune since

speaker: VIOLA
text: Hath been between this lady and this lord.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: So comes it, lady, you have been mistook.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: But nature to her bias drew in that.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: You would have been contracted to a maid.

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: Nor are you therein, by my life, deceived:

speaker: SEBASTIAN
text: You are betrothed both to a maid and man.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Be not amazed; right noble is his blood.

speaker: ORSINO
text: If this be so, as yet the glass seems true,

speaker: ORSINO
text: I shall have share in this most happy wrack.—

speaker: ORSINO
text: Boy, thou hast said to me a thousand times

speaker: ORSINO
text: Thou never shouldst love woman like to me.

speaker: VIOLA
text: And all those sayings will I overswear,

speaker: VIOLA
text: And all those swearings keep as true in soul

speaker: VIOLA
text: As doth that orbèd continent the fire

speaker: VIOLA
text: That severs day from night.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Give me thy hand,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And let me see thee in thy woman’s weeds.

speaker: VIOLA
text: The Captain that did bring me first on shore

speaker: VIOLA
text: Hath my maid’s garments. He, upon some action,

speaker: VIOLA
text: Is now in durance at Malvolio’s suit,

speaker: VIOLA
text: A gentleman and follower of my lady’s.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: He shall enlarge him.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Fetch Malvolio hither.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: And yet, alas, now I remember me,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: They say, poor gentleman, he’s much distract.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: A most extracting frenzy of mine own

speaker: OLIVIA
text: From my remembrance clearly banished his.

speaker: OLIVIA
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Fool
                .
                 
              How does he, sirrah?

speaker: FOOL
text: Truly, madam, he holds Beelzebub at the stave’s

speaker: FOOL
text: end as well as a man in his case may do. Has here

speaker: FOOL
text: writ a letter to you. I should have given ’t you today

speaker: FOOL
text: morning. But as a madman’s epistles are no gospels,

speaker: FOOL
text: so it skills not much when they are delivered.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Open ’t and read it.

speaker: FOOL
text: Look then to be well edified, when the Fool

speaker: FOOL
text:
delivers the madman. 
                He
                 
                reads
                .
                 
              
                By
                 
                the
                 
                Lord
                ,
                
                madam
                —

speaker: OLIVIA
text: How now, art thou mad?

speaker: FOOL
text: No, madam, I do but read madness. An your

speaker: FOOL
text: Ladyship will have it as it ought to be, you must

speaker: FOOL
text: allow vox.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Prithee, read i’ thy right wits.

speaker: FOOL
text: So I do, madonna. But to read his right wits is to

speaker: FOOL
text: read thus. Therefore, perpend, my princess, and

speaker: FOOL
text: give ear.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Read it you, sirrah.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Did he write this?

speaker: FOOL
text: Ay, madam.

speaker: ORSINO
text: This savors not much of distraction.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: See him delivered, Fabian. Bring him hither.

speaker: OLIVIA
text:
To
                 
                Orsino
                .
                 
              My lord, so please you, these things further thought on,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: To think me as well a sister as a wife,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: One day shall crown th’ alliance on ’t, so please you,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Here at my house, and at my proper cost.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Madam, I am most apt t’ embrace your offer.

speaker: ORSINO
text:
To
                 
                Viola
                .
                 
              Your master quits you; and for your service done him,

speaker: ORSINO
text: So much against the mettle of your sex,

speaker: ORSINO
text: So far beneath your soft and tender breeding,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And since you called me “master” for so long,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Here is my hand. You shall from this time be

speaker: ORSINO
text: Your master’s mistress.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: A sister! You are she.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Is this the madman?

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Ay, my lord, this same.—

speaker: OLIVIA
text: How now, Malvolio?

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Madam, you have done me wrong,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Notorious wrong.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Have I, Malvolio? No.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Lady, you have. Pray you peruse that letter.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: You must not now deny it is your hand.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Write from it if you can, in hand or phrase,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Or say ’tis not your seal, not your invention.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: You can say none of this. Well, grant it then,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: And tell me, in the modesty of honor,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Why you have given me such clear lights of favor?

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Bade me come smiling and cross-gartered to you,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: To put on yellow stockings, and to frown

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Upon Sir Toby and the lighter people?

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: And, acting this in an obedient hope,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Why have you suffered me to be imprisoned,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: Kept in a dark house, visited by the priest,

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: And made the most notorious geck and gull

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: That e’er invention played on? Tell me why.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Alas, Malvolio, this is not my writing,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Though I confess much like the character.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: But out of question, ’tis Maria’s hand.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: And now I do bethink me, it was she

speaker: OLIVIA
text: First told me thou wast mad; then cam’st in smiling,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: And in such forms which here were presupposed

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Upon thee in the letter. Prithee, be content.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: This practice hath most shrewdly passed upon thee.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: But when we know the grounds and authors of it,

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Thou shalt be both the plaintiff and the judge

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Of thine own cause.

speaker: FABIAN
text: Good madam, hear me speak,

speaker: FABIAN
text: And let no quarrel nor no brawl to come

speaker: FABIAN
text: Taint the condition of this present hour,

speaker: FABIAN
text: Which I have wondered at. In hope it shall not,

speaker: FABIAN
text: Most freely I confess, myself and Toby

speaker: FABIAN
text: Set this device against Malvolio here,

speaker: FABIAN
text: Upon some stubborn and uncourteous parts

speaker: FABIAN
text: We had conceived against him. Maria writ

speaker: FABIAN
text: The letter at Sir Toby’s great importance,

speaker: FABIAN
text: In recompense whereof he hath married her.

speaker: FABIAN
text: How with a sportful malice it was followed

speaker: FABIAN
text: May rather pluck on laughter than revenge,

speaker: FABIAN
text: If that the injuries be justly weighed

speaker: FABIAN
text: That have on both sides passed.

speaker: OLIVIA
text: Alas, poor fool, how have they baffled thee!

speaker: FOOL
text: Why, “some are born great, some achieve greatness,

speaker: FOOL
text: and some have greatness thrown upon them.”

speaker: FOOL
text: I was one, sir, in this interlude, one Sir Topas, sir,

speaker: FOOL
text: but that’s all one. “By the Lord, Fool, I am not

speaker: FOOL
text: mad”—but, do you remember “Madam, why laugh

speaker: FOOL
text: you at such a barren rascal; an you smile not, he’s

speaker: FOOL
text: gagged”? And thus the whirligig of time brings in

speaker: FOOL
text: his revenges.

speaker: MALVOLIO
text: I’ll be revenged on the whole pack of you!

speaker: OLIVIA
text: He hath been most notoriously abused.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Pursue him and entreat him to a peace.

speaker: ORSINO
text: He hath not told us of the Captain yet.

speaker: ORSINO
text: When that is known, and golden time convents,

speaker: ORSINO
text: A solemn combination shall be made

speaker: ORSINO
text: Of our dear souls.—Meantime, sweet sister,

speaker: ORSINO
text: We will not part from hence.—Cesario, come,

speaker: ORSINO
text: For so you shall be while you are a man.

speaker: ORSINO
text: But when in other habits you are seen,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Orsino’s mistress, and his fancy’s queen.

speaker: FOOL
text: When that I was and a little tiny boy,

speaker: FOOL
text: With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,

speaker: FOOL
text: A foolish thing was but a toy,

speaker: FOOL
text: For the rain it raineth every day.

speaker: FOOL
text: But when I came to man’s estate,

speaker: FOOL
text: With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,

speaker: FOOL
text: ’Gainst knaves and thieves men shut their gate,

speaker: FOOL
text: For the rain it raineth every day.

speaker: FOOL
text: But when I came, alas, to wive,

speaker: FOOL
text: With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,

speaker: FOOL
text: By swaggering could I never thrive,

speaker: FOOL
text: For the rain it raineth every day.

speaker: FOOL
text: But when I came unto my beds,

speaker: FOOL
text: With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,

speaker: FOOL
text: With tosspots still had drunken heads,

speaker: FOOL
text: For the rain it raineth every day.

speaker: FOOL
text: A great while ago the world begun,

speaker: FOOL
text: With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,

speaker: FOOL
text: But that’s all one, our play is done,

speaker: FOOL
text: And we’ll strive to please you every day.
